title: HumanEgo: Zero-Shot Robot Learning from Minutes of Human Egocentric Videos
summary: HumanEgo is a framework that enables robots to learn manipulation skills from brief human egocentric video recordings without any robot demonstrations. It bridges the embodiment gap through entity-level representations of hand-object interaction and employs flow-matching policies with auxiliary training objectives. With only 30 minutes of human video per task, the system achieves 92.5% average success across real-world tasks, outperforming matched-time robot teleoperation by 41%, and generalizes zero-shot across different robot platforms, cameras, and environments.
